chapter_title.en: Chapter 68 – Goodly relationship with the relatives

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (387/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (387/ 8)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) صِلَةُ الرَّحِمِ تُهَوِّنُ الْحِسَابَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ هِيَ مَنْسَأَةٌ فِي الْعُمُرِ وَ تَقِي مَصَارِعَ السُّوءِ وَ صَدَقَةُ اللَّيْلِ تُطْفِئُ غَضَبَ الرَّبِّ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Abdul Samad Bin Bashir who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The goodly relationships would ease the Reckoning on the Day of Judgement, and it is a delayer is the (termination of the) life-span and saves from the evil misfortunes; and the giving of the charity at night extinguishes the Anger of the Lord<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 32. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from ‘Abd al-Samad ibn Bashir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Maintaining good relations with relatives eases off the giving of accounts on the Day of Judgment, delays the coming of death and safeguards against misfortunes. Giving charity at night extinguishes the wrath of the Lord.’”
